calendar.matters:
PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Jun-01-2015 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together: PES-15-298554 Estate of Lafayette Jenkins and PES-15-298630 Estate of Margaret Jenkins.
Appearances: Melvin Hodges for (Petitioner) Lottie Steib. Randy Schmidt for Earl Watson.

Mr. Hodges reported that he received the documents on Friday, that he has taken a cursory look at them, that the decedents died weeks apart, that there is no ambiguity of who died first, that the wife died first and that there is no basis to revert the estate to her. Mr. Schmidt reported that he could not find anything under P.C. 6402.5, that there is no dispute as to who died first, cited Sears vs. Rule regarding joint tenancy deeds and that there is a friend disputing the signature on Lafayette's will as not being his. Mr. Hodges stated that he will file a declaration and that if there is an issue with the will there should be a will contest. Mr. Schmidt stated that Mr. Hodges was to have served Margaret's side with notice of the proceedings but that he does not think he did so. The Court stated that the 298630 action (Estate of Margaret Jenkins) will go off calendar. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary (298554, Estate of Lafayette Jenkins) was continued to July 27, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for a declaration regarding the heirs to be filed and for everyone to receive notice.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar May-06-2015 Continued To Jun-01-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together: PES-15-298554 Estate of Lafayette Jenkins and PES-15-298630 Estate of Margaret Jenkins.
Appearances: Randy Schmidt for (petitioner/objector) Earl Watson. Melvin Hodges for Lottie Steib.
The Court asked if the 298630 action (estate of Margaret Jenkins) could go off calendar. Mr. Schmidt stated that he just found a 58 page ALR and that research would need to be done to see if the estate is appropriate. Mr. Hodges stated that the pre-deceased spouse meant for her belongings to be rolled into Mr. Jenkins estate and that there is only a need for Mr. Jenkins estate. Mr. Schmidt reported that the filing fees have been taken care of, that notice has been given on Margaret's estate and that he does not believe Mr. Hodges gave notice in the Lafayette estate regarding Margaret. Mr. Hodges stated that he would clear up any deficiencies regarding notice. The Court requested that Mr. Schmidt submit a brief regarding his findings and that depending on the findings the Margaret Jenkins estate may go off calendar. The petition for probate of will and for letters testamentary (Lafayette Jenkins) and the petition for letters of administration with will annexed (Margaret Jenkins) were continued to June 1, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Mr. Schmidt to file a brief.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Lopes. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
